name: xbrl

mif.reason: Inline XBRL (iXBRL) is a machine-readable regulatory serialization orthogonal to MIF Level-3 markdown structure. It is rendered directly from the SOURCES — the surviving findings (typically a regulatory-disclosure artifact) and their primary-source citations — never from a rendered report, and carries the topic's MIF Level-1 identity in the document's XHTML head metadata. The citation-leak gate still applies; the MIF Level-3 source of truth lives in the generic report channel.

description: Render surviving findings (typically a regulatory-disclosure artifact) into an inline XBRL (iXBRL) document directly from the SOURCES. Tags facts with xbrli:context / xbrli:unit and taxonomy concepts inside a human-readable XHTML shell, anchored to the current SEC inline-XBRL taxonomy. Never built from a rendered report.
